meaning: adj.不通风的,空气不流畅的
examples: That explosion was triggered by an accumulation of gas in an unventilated tunnel.
In may, he said, miners were dismayed to discover that 59 mules had died from unventilated mine gas.
Thus it was this time not heaven, but the upper space of an unventilated concert-hall, that sent down snow.
As for the dorm, an employee said eight to 10 people live in a room smaller than 220 sq ft, which is cramped and unventilated.
The buildings were considered pioneers in the "light-court" model of tenement development, intended as an alternative to the dark and unventilated tenements common at the time.
